message:
chore(beads): close ft-oqfsx — database_stats Result propagation shipped

Signature change + 9 caller updates + 3 fixture tests landed at 67b6dc359.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -456,7 +456,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-ombfl.7","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-fixture] Cursor states fixture: block / underline / beam, with/without focus, blink-disabled","description":"# GPU fixture — cursor states

## Background
Cursor rendering is one of the highest-visibility regression
surfaces. Block, underline, beam shapes; focused vs unfocused
appearance; blink behavior. Every change to the cursor shader risks
breaking one of these states.

## Why this matters
- Users rely on visual cursor cues for orientation. A bug here is
  immediately visible and frustrating.
- Cursor color, blending, and contrast (against arbitrary
  backgrounds) are subtle; goldens catch sneaky regressions.

## Scope
Author 6 fixtures under tests/golden/gpu/:
1. **cursor-block-focused/**: cursor in default block shape, pane
   focused, on a normal background.
2. **cursor-block-unfocused/**: same but pane unfocused (cursor
   typically becomes a hollow outline).
3. **cursor-underline-focused/**: underline cursor shape.
4. **cursor-beam-focused/**: vertical beam cursor.
5. **cursor-on-emoji/**: cursor positioned over a wide emoji glyph
   (verifies cursor still visually clear).
6. **cursor-on-cjk/**: cursor over a CJK fullwidth glyph (cursor
   should span the full cell width).

All fixtures: blink DISABLED (deterministic).

## Out of scope
- Blinking-cursor frame capture (would need timing — separate bead).
- Custom cursor colors via OSC sequences — could be a follow-on.

## Approach
1. Author 6 input.json files specifying cursor type + position +
   underlying cell.
2. Each meta.json specifies cursor_blink=false.
3. Generate goldens via --update-goldens.
4. Manually inspect: each cursor type visibly distinct.
5. Commit.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 6 fixtures committed.
- Tests pass.
- Each fixture's golden shows the cursor in the documented shape.
- cursor-on-emoji and cursor-on-cjk visibly span the full glyph
  cell.

## Testing
- Fixture self-test.
- Mutation: change cursor type from block to beam; confirm fixture
  fails.
- Mutation: change focus state; confirm focused vs unfocused
  fixtures both fail when focus value is wrong.

## Risks / considerations
- Cursor color often tracks the foreground/background palette. Pin
  the palette in meta.json so these tests aren't affected by theme.
- For cursor-on-emoji: the cursor should not OBSCURE the emoji
  but should be visible. Both states are valid renders depending on
  blend mode; pick one and pin it.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- fixture

## References
- GPU_RENDERER (prerequisite).

 {"id":"ft-ombfl.8","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-fixture] Selection highlight + overlays fixture: char/word/line/rectangular selection, search hits, lens overlays","description":"# GPU fixture — selection highlight + overlays

## Background
Selection rendering and overlay layers are blend-heavy — easy to
break in renderer changes that touch alpha or layering order.

## Why this matters
- Selection is the most-frequent user interaction with cursor-on-text.
- Search hit highlights, error squiggles, and floating panel
  overlays all use the same blending substrate.

## Scope
Author 5 fixtures under tests/golden/gpu/:
1. **selection-char/**: a multi-line character selection.
2. **selection-word/**: word-mode selection (double-click style).
3. **selection-line/**: line-mode selection (triple-click).
4. **selection-rectangular/**: block selection across columns.
5. **overlay-search-hits/**: a search query with multiple highlighted
   hits in different colors (e.g., current hit + other matches).

## Out of scope
- Animated selection extension (drag preview) — out of scope for
  static fixtures.
- Multi-cursor selection (separate bead if a feature later).

## Approach
1. Author 5 input.json files specifying selection bounds + style.
2. Each fixture with deterministic palette (foreground/background +
   selection-bg color pinned).
3. Generate goldens.
4. Manually inspect: selection bg covers exactly the selected
   cells, no spillover, text remains readable.
5. Commit.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 5 fixtures committed.
- Tests pass.
- Selection bounds are pixel-correct.
- Text contrast against selection bg passes a manual readability
  check (no white-on-white).

## Testing
- Fixture self-test.
- Mutation: shift selection by 1 char; confirm fixture fails with
  diff showing edge-cell change.
- Mutation: change selection alpha; confirm fixture fails.

## Risks / considerations
- Selection rendering may differ between platforms (macOS uses
  blend-mode multiply by convention; Linux software-render may
  approximate). Pin the blend mode in meta.json or use a single
  CI runner.
- Rectangular selection is rarer; document its semantics in the
  fixture's input.json.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- fixture

## References
- GPU_RENDERER (prerequisite).

 {"id":"ft-ombfl.9","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-fixture] Multi-pane layout fixture: split divider geometry + focused/unfocused borders + 3+ panes","description":"# GPU fixture — multi-pane layout

## Background
ft-1memj.28 closed the divider-geometry test (bf9db5d5) — that test
pinned a single split's geometry. This fixture goes broader:
multi-pane layouts (2/3/4+ panes), focused vs unfocused border
treatment, and the cell-padding around dividers.

## Why this matters
- frankenterm is a swarm-native terminal — multi-pane layouts are
  the dominant use case (the 2026-04-27 swarm itself ran with 7
  panes). Regressions in multi-pane render are core breakage.
- Divider rendering is shared across split, tab, and floating
  surfaces — a fix in one can break the others.

## Scope
Author 5 fixtures under tests/golden/gpu/:
1. **multipane-2split-h/**: horizontal split, 2 panes side-by-side,
   left focused.
2. **multipane-2split-v/**: vertical split, 2 panes stacked, top
   focused.
3. **multipane-grid-4/**: 2x2 grid of panes, top-left focused.
4. **multipane-deep-nested/**: 5-pane layout with mixed splits
   (test divider corners and T-joins).
5. **multipane-floating-overlay/**: a floating pane on top of a
   normal split layout.

All fixtures: cursor blink off, deterministic content per pane.

## Out of scope
- Resize animation (static frames only).
- Tab bar rendering (separate fixture if needed).

## Approach
1. Author input.json for each, with explicit pane tree + focus state.
2. meta.json specifies viewport size, dpi, divider color.
3. Generate goldens.
4. Manual inspection: divider lines connect at corners, focused
   border is visibly distinct, padding is consistent.
5. Commit.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 5 fixtures committed.
- Tests pass.
- Divider corners are pixel-aligned (T-joins, +-joins, L-joins).
- Focused vs unfocused border is visibly distinct in goldens.
- Floating overlay correctly shadows underlying panes if shadow
  is enabled.

## Testing
- Fixture self-test.
- Mutation: change focus from one pane to another; confirm fixture
  fails with diff showing border swap.
- Mutation: shift one pane by 1 cell; confirm divider misalignment
  catches.
- Logging: per-fixture render_ms; multi-pane should scale roughly
  linearly with pane count (sanity check; not a perf gate).

## Risks / considerations
- Float-overlay shadow rendering may include per-frame randomness
  (e.g., dithering). Pin a deterministic shadow algorithm or
  disable shadows in meta.json.
- Deep-nested layout's divider intersections are the trickiest;
  triple-check corner pixel by manual inspection before committing.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- fixture

## References
- ft-1memj.28 / bf9db5d5 (divider single-split geometry).
- GPU_RENDERER (prerequisite).
- ft-ombfl (parent).","notes":"## Polish pass round 3 (concrete resize sequence)

### multipane-resize-static-snapshot fixture sequence
The previous polish added this fixture but didn't specify the
resize sequence. Concrete values:
- Initial viewport: 80x24 (standard terminal).
- Step 1: 100x30 (wider + taller).
- Step 2: 120x40 (continue scaling).
- Step 3: 90x35 (shrink width).
- Step 4 (final, captured): 100x35.
Each step exercises a different resize concern:
- 80→100x24→30: pure growth, atlas may need to add new lines.
- 100→120x30→40: aggressive growth, glyph cache may evict.
- 120→90x40→35: width shrink, must re-flow content.
- 90→100x35: final stable state, captured as golden.

Layout: 2x2 grid of panes throughout the resize. Each pane has
deterministic content: pane 0 = 5-line ASCII, pane 1 = 3-line
CJK, pane 2 = box-drawing table, pane 3 = colored selection.
This way the resize exercises ALL render paths (text, CJK,
box-drawing, selection).

### Per-pane render_ms in fixture metadata
Update fixture's expected.json schema to record per-pane
render_ms in addition to whole-frame:
```json
{
  "fixture_name": "multipane-resize-static-snapshot",
  "panes": [
    {"id": 0, "expected_render_ms_max": 5},
    {"id": 1, "expected_render_ms_max": 8},
    ...
  ]
}
```
Catches single-pane regressions hidden in whole-frame averages.

### Cursor blink off in ALL panes (re-emphasized)
Confirmed for all 6 fixtures in this bead. Add a meta.json
field `cursor_blink_disabled_globally: true` and reject any
fixture missing it (loader-level check).

### Total fixtures in this bead = 6 (was 5+1)
- multipane-2split-h
- multipane-2split-v
- multipane-grid-4
- multipane-deep-nested
- multipane-floating-overlay
- multipane-resize-static-snapshot","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_4","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:08:28.280405Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:59:32.189717Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T04:59:32.189472Z","close_reason":"Added six multi-pane GPU golden fixtures; rch headless GPU regression passed 24/24","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fixture","gpu-harness","postswarm-2026-04-27"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.9","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:08:28.280405Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.9","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:09:07.059322Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-oqfsx","title":"[multi-pass][pass-3] storage.rs:3863 database_stats(): silent SQL errors via .ok() chain — ft doctor reports green on corrupt DB","description":"## Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:3863`:

```rust
pub fn database_stats(db_path: &Path, retention_days: u32) -> DbStatsReport {
    ...
    let pane_stats = {
        let mut stmt = conn
            .prepare(\"SELECT ... FROM output_segments s ...\")
            .ok();                          // ← swallows prepare error
        match stmt.as_mut() {
            Some(s) => s
                .query_map([], |row| { ... })
                .ok()                       // ← swallows query error
                .map(|rows| rows.filter_map(|r| r.ok()).collect())   // ← swallows row error
                .unwrap_or_default(),       // ← swallows everything else
            None => vec![],                 // ← prepare-failed → empty Vec
        }
    };
    // identical pattern at 3944-3963 for event_type stats
    ...
}
```

The function returns `DbStatsReport` (NOT `Result<DbStatsReport>`).
ALL SQL errors — prepare failures, query failures, row deserialize
failures — are silently converted to empty `Vec<PaneStats>` /
`Vec<EventTypeStats>`. The caller has no way to distinguish
"DB has zero panes" from "DB schema is corrupt and prepare failed."

Per the function's apparent role (called by `ft doctor` JSON output
and the diagnostic surface), this is a real observability bug:

- **Operator runs `ft doctor --json` after a partial schema
  migration that left a table missing.**
- **The `prepare(\"SELECT ... FROM output_segments\")` returns Err
  because the table doesn't exist.**
- `.ok()` converts to `None`.
- `match stmt.as_mut() { ... None => vec![] }`.
- Doctor reports `pane_stats: []` (zero panes).
- Operator reads "looks fine — no panes captured yet."
- Real diagnosis: the recorder has been silently failing for hours
  because the `output_segments` table is gone.

## Why this is a real defect

This violates the skill's Pass 3 contract (error-path correctness):
**SQL errors are real signals**. The current code converts them to
"no data" — semantically the same as a successful empty query.

The function's `unwrap_or(0)` pattern at line 3902 (the `count = ...
unwrap_or(0)` for table sizing) has the same issue: a table-missing
error gets normalized to zero.

This compounds the security audit's ft-3se13 finding (decision log
unredacted) — the surfaces operators rely on to verify ft is
healthy give false-green.

## Suggested fix

Three options (preferring B):

A. **Change return type to `Result<DbStatsReport>`.** Most invasive,
   forces every caller to handle. Probably overkill for the doctor
   surface where graceful degradation is wanted.

B. **Introduce per-stat error fields**:

   ```rust
   pub struct DbStatsReport {
       pub pane_stats: Vec<PaneStats>,
       pub pane_stats_error: Option<String>,    // ← NEW
       pub event_types: Vec<EventTypeStats>,
       pub event_types_error: Option<String>,   // ← NEW
       ...
   }
   ```

   Each `.ok()` site captures the error message into the corresponding
   `_error` field. `ft doctor` JSON output exposes these; operators
   can grep for non-null errors.

C. **Log + telemetry counter on every swallowed error**:

   ```rust
   .map_err(|e| {
       tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"database_stats: prepare failed\");
       crate::telemetry::counter!(\"database_stats.error\").increment(1);
       e
   })
   .ok()
   ```

Option B + C combined is best: structured field + observability counter.

## Acceptance criteria

- `DbStatsReport` exposes a per-stat error field (or equivalent)
  for every `.ok()` swallow site.
- `ft doctor --json` output includes the error fields when set.
- A regression test simulates a missing-table state and asserts the
  error is surfaced (not silently zero).
- Telemetry counter increments on every swallowed error.

## References

- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:3863-3963` (database_stats
  function body with the .ok() chain)
- README §Diagnostics (ft doctor is the marketed surface)
- ft-40k1o (sibling: ft doctor golden coverage gap — this defect is
  exactly what the goldens would catch if they existed)
- Skill: multi-pass-bug-hunting Pass 3 ("error-path correctness")

Filed by cc_1 multi-pass bug hunt, Pass 3, 2026-04-28.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T10:19:06.038972Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:08:48.814500Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Filed by cc_1 multi-pass bug hunt, Pass 3, 2026-04-28.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T10:19:06.038972Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:18:59.099252Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T20:18:59.099070Z","close_reason":"Shipped (commit 67b6dc359). Signature changed: database_stats(...) -> Result<DbStatsReport, StorageError>. Every per-table COUNT(*), each prepare/query_map, each row collect now propagates failure as StorageError::Database with table/phase context, plus a structured tracing::error! event with db_path. Connection::open failure stays a non-fatal Ok-with-suggestion branch (so callers can distinguish 'DB not yet created' from 'DB exists but queries failed'). Updated 9 callers: ft db stats CLI (main.rs) emits JSON-or-plain error + exit 1; cleanup.rs (7 sites) and tests/cleanup_labruntime.rs (7 sites) use .expect for test contract. 3 new fixture tests in storage::db_check_repair_tests pin the contract: healthy DB returns Ok, missing-DB path returns Ok with 'could not be opened' suggestion, dropped-events-table returns Err with 'events' in the message. All 3 pass under --no-default-features. ft binary compiles clean with default features.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-otfxs","title":"[ft-t2d70.2] extract frankenterm-core-config-types leaf crate","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:22:45.766011Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:38:35.541080Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:38:35.540713Z","close_reason":"Extracted frankenterm-core-config-types leaf crate at HEAD 01438b01 in ~10 min. tuning_config.rs (1384 LOC, operator-tunable [tuning] section types) moved cleanly — zero crate::* deps. Re-exported via pub use frankenterm_core_config_types::tuning_config from frankenterm-core/src/lib.rs so all in-tree call sites (policy.rs, runtime.rs, recorder_audit.rs, agent_correlator.rs, ipc.rs + 5 test files) compile unchanged. Leaf crate: 0.42s clean check. NOT extracted: config.rs (7293 LOC, 135 cross-cluster refs to ~30 modules), config_profiles.rs (callbacks to crate::config), agent_config_templates.rs (crate::agent_provider dep) — each needs its own prerequisite work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-otfxs","depends_on_id":"ft-t2d70","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:22:45.766011Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-oujyv","title":"Preserve leftmost-first semantics across split trigger automatons","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:05:04.287556Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:17:48.115684Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:17:48.115421Z","close_reason":"Already landed on main as 906ff02d with the leftmost-first dedup fix and overlapping exact/nocase regression tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-p2bjm","title":"[fuzz] add replay::Recording::from_bytes fuzz target for .war recording binary format","description":"Surface: crates/frankenterm-core/src/replay.rs:128 — pub fn Recording::from_bytes(data: &[u8]) and the internal parse_frame at :36. This is the binary .war recording file loader. Attack surface: any command that opens a user-supplied recording path (ft replay load, session restore, CI-fed fixtures). Oversize cap at 256 MiB was added in 3a0cac70 (security) but no coverage-guided harness exercises the parse_frame byte machinery.

Proposed harness: fuzz/fuzz_targets/replay_recording_from_bytes.rs
  #![no_main]
  use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;
  use frankenterm_core::replay::Recording;
  fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
      if data.len() > 8_000_000 { return; }
      let _ = Recording::from_bytes(data);
  });

Corpus seeds: fuzz/corpus/replay_recording_from_bytes/ — (a) empty, (b) single Output frame with 1-byte payload, (c) multi-frame recording with interleaved OSC133 markers + PaneOutput from an existing war fixture, (d) frame claiming huge payload_len (boundary-of-bounds), (e) truncated frame header mid-read, (f) zero-duration recording.

Oracle: no panic, no allocation explosion, no infinite loop (tracked by libfuzzer timeout). Structure-aware Arbitrary impl on RecordingFrame could accelerate discovery after initial coverage plateau.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:49:32.230576Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:29:40.334910Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:29:40.334105Z","close_reason":"Already landed in 94f171e7; replay_recording_from_bytes is present in fuzz/Cargo.toml and cargo fuzz list.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
